S.FORM: sesane ka pito na koli'i ko mowali gako.
S.PHON: səsanə ka pito na koliʔi ko mowali gako.
S.FORM: sesane ka pito na koli'i ko mowali gako.
S.PHON: səsanə ka pito na koliʔi ko mowali gako.
S.TRANSL: 我今天七點到學校。
I arrived at school at seven today.

S.FORM: amwanga na zikang, singsi ka la oda'ane mwa tasolasolatane, pang-cange la 'apobaka, "'id'iya! sokoba!"
S.PHON: amwaŋa na zikaŋ, siŋsi ka la odaʔanə mwa tasolasolatanə, paŋ-ʦaŋə la ʔapobaka, "ʔidʔija! sokoba!"
S.FORM: amwanga na zikang, singsi ka la oda'ane mwa tasolasolatane, pang-cange la 'apobaka, "'id'iya! sokoba!"
S.PHON: amwaŋa na zikaŋ, siŋsi ka la odaʔanə mwa tasolasolatanə, paŋ-ʦaŋə la ʔapobaka, "ʔidʔija! sokoba!"
S.TRANSL: 時間到了，老師走進教室，班長說:＂起立!敬禮!＂
It's time for class , the teacher walked into the classroom, and the monitor said: "Stand up! Salute!"

S.FORM: 'akwa draw kiname 'apobaka, "singsiyane ipoingow nyaso."
S.PHON: ʔakwa ɖaw kinamə ʔapobaka, "siŋsijanə ipoiŋow njaso."
S.FORM: 'akwa draw kiname 'apobaka, "singsiyane ipoingow nyaso."
S.PHON: ʔakwa ɖaw kinamə ʔapobaka, "siŋsijanə ipoiŋow njaso."
S.TRANSL: 我們大聲的說:＂老師好。＂
We said loudly, "Hi, teacher."

S.FORM: kosolatiname ka paigo'o namiya kisingisina sasysipi na wakacaisisiyne.
S.PHON: kosolatinamə ka paigoʔo namija kisiŋisina sasjsipi na wakaʦaisisijnə.
S.FORM: kosolatiname ka paigo'o namiya kisingisina sasysipi na wakacaisisiyne.
S.PHON: kosolatinamə ka paigoʔo namija kisiŋisina sasjsipi na wakaʦaisisijnə.
S.TRANSL: 上課的時候，老師教我們數字的族語。
In class, the teacher taught us the Rukai language of numbers.

S.FORM: 'angopapa kiname singsiane wasyasipi, wathenay kiname na wasyasipi na thenay.
S.PHON: ʔaŋopapa kinamə siŋsianə wasjasipi, waθənaj kinamə na wasjasipi na θənaj.
S.FORM: 'angopapa kiname singsiane wasyasipi, wathenay kiname na wasyasipi na thenay.
S.PHON: ʔaŋopapa kinamə siŋsianə wasjasipi, waθənaj kinamə na wasjasipi na θənaj.
S.TRANSL: 我們跟著老師唸，我們唱了數字歌。
We followed the teacher to read, we sang the number song.

S.FORM: madrao igo'ili na wa kacaisisiyane.
S.PHON: maɖao igoʔili na wa kaʦaisisijanə.
S.FORM: madrao igo'ili na wa kacaisisiyane.
S.PHON: maɖao igoʔili na wa kaʦaisisijanə.
S.TRANSL: 我學會好多族語。
I have learned many Rukai languages.

S.FORM: pava'iyane kakemwagako pava'iyane kake kicawane kwacaisisiyane. 'apobaka mekake pabiciingi titinane.
S.PHON: pavaʔijanə kakəmwagako pavaʔijanə kakə kiʦawanə kwaʦaisisijanə. ʔapobaka məkakə pabiʦiiŋi titinanə.
S.FORM: pava'iyane kakemwagako pava'iyane kake kicawane kwacaisisiyane. 'apobaka mekake pabiciingi titinane.
S.PHON: pavaʔijanə kakəmwagako pavaʔijanə kakə kiʦawanə kwaʦaisisijanə. ʔapobaka məkakə pabiʦiiŋi titinanə.
S.TRANSL: 我天天上學，我天天學族語，我要講給媽媽聽。
I go to school every day, I learn the Rukai language every day, and I want to tell my mother.

S.FORM: acilika takanaw.
S.PHON: aʦilika takanaw.
S.FORM: acilika takanaw.
S.PHON: aʦilika takanaw.
S.TRANSL: 我的名字是takanaw。
My name is takanaw.

S.FORM: acini ki tatara ka Tanobake.
S.PHON: aʦini ki tata*a ka tanobakə.
S.FORM: acini ki tatara ka Tanobake.
S.PHON: aʦini ki tata*a ka tanobakə.
S.TRANSL: 我爸爸的名字是Tanobake。
My dad's name is Tanobake.

S.FORM: acini ki titina ka 'Ipolo.
S.PHON: aʦini ki titina ka ʔipolo.
S.FORM: acini ki titina ka 'Ipolo.
S.PHON: aʦini ki titina ka ʔipolo.
S.TRANSL: 我媽媽的名字是’Ipolo。
My mother's name is 'Ipolo.

S.FORM: na kay cicavilika wata ma ngesale ngana civilili.
S.PHON: na kaj ʦiʦavilika wata ma ŋəsalə ŋana ʦivilili.
S.FORM: na kay cicavilika wata ma ngesale ngana civilili.
S.PHON: na kaj ʦiʦavilika wata ma ŋəsalə ŋana ʦivilili.
S.TRANSL: 我今年10歲。
I am 10 years old.

S.FORM: 'iyakay nakowa 'asaseane na sawalay na kaka acinika gilagilaw.
S.PHON: ʔijakaj nakowa ʔasasəanə na sawalaj na kaka aʦinika gilagilaw.
S.FORM: 'iyakay nakowa 'asaseane na sawalay na kaka acinika gilagilaw.
S.PHON: ʔijakaj nakowa ʔasasəanə na sawalaj na kaka aʦinika gilagilaw.
S.TRANSL: 我有一個哥哥，他叫gilagilaw，他今年11歲。
I have a brother, his name is gilagilaw, he is 11 years old.

S.FORM: ta'aese kiname 'ikay na gako sola solate.
S.PHON: taʔaəsə kinamə ʔikaj na gako sola solatə.
S.FORM: ta'aese kiname 'ikay na gako sola solate.
S.PHON: taʔaəsə kinamə ʔikaj na gako sola solatə.
S.TRANSL: 我們在同一間學校讀書。
We study in the same school.

S.FORM: kaka si kake ka madalame kiname the nay.
S.PHON: kaka si kakə ka madalamə kinamə θə naj.
S.FORM: kaka si kake ka madalame kiname the nay.
S.PHON: kaka si kakə ka madalamə kinamə θə naj.
S.TRANSL: 我和哥哥都喜歡唱歌。
Both my brother and I like to sing.

S.FORM: madalame kake lwangolwan. ta'isasadrane ka 'angese kiname ka thakada'ane mwa lwangolangoy.
S.PHON: madalamə kakə lwaŋolwan. taʔisasaɖanə ka ʔaŋəsə kinamə ka θakadaʔanə mwa lwaŋolaŋoj.
S.FORM: madalame kake lwangolwan. ta'isasadrane ka 'angese kiname ka thakada'ane mwa lwangolangoy.
S.PHON: madalamə kakə lwaŋolwan. taʔisasaɖanə ka ʔaŋəsə kinamə ka θakadaʔanə mwa lwaŋolaŋoj.
S.TRANSL: 我喜歡游泳，放假的時候，我們全家會一起去游泳。
I like swimming. During the holidays, our whole family go swimming together.

S.FORM: madalame kake na ba'owane na la'osay angese kiname thenay lwangolangoy.
S.PHON: madalamə kakə na baʔowanə na laʔosaj aŋəsə kinamə θənaj lwaŋolaŋoj.
S.FORM: madalame kake na ba'owane na la'osay angese kiname thenay lwangolangoy.
S.PHON: madalamə kakə na baʔowanə na laʔosaj aŋəsə kinamə θənaj lwaŋolaŋoj.
S.TRANSL: 我喜歡認識新朋友，我們可以一起唱歌和游泳。
I love meeting new people, we can sing and swim together.

S.FORM: swadrekay kake ka valavalake.
S.PHON: swaɖəkaj kakə ka valavalakə.
S.FORM: swadrekay kake ka valavalake.
S.PHON: swaɖəkaj kakə ka valavalakə.
S.TRANSL: 我是魯凱族的小孩。
I am an valavalake child.

S.FORM: da'aniname ka iykay kongadavane.
S.PHON: daʔaninamə ka ijkaj koŋadavanə.
S.FORM: da'aniname ka iykay kongadavane.
S.PHON: daʔaninamə ka ijkaj koŋadavanə.
S.TRANSL: 我們家住在多納。
Our family lives in kongadavane.

S.FORM: da'aniname ka ta'aima na cwao.
S.PHON: daʔaninamə ka taʔaima na ʦwao.
S.FORM: da'aniname ka ta'aima na cwao.
S.PHON: daʔaninamə ka taʔaima na ʦwao.
S.TRANSL: 我們家共有五個人。
There are five people in our family.

S.FORM: da'aniname ka iyakay 'a too na lamengame.
S.PHON: daʔaninamə ka ijakaj ʔa too na laməŋamə.
S.FORM: da'aniname ka iyakay 'a too na lamengame.
S.PHON: daʔaninamə ka ijakaj ʔa too na laməŋamə.
S.TRANSL: 我們家有三個兄弟姊妹。
There are three brothers and sisters in our family.

S.FORM: kake si tatava, titina, kaka sawalay. si kaka 'abayane.
S.PHON: kakə si tatava, titina, kaka sawalaj. si kaka ʔabajanə.
S.FORM: kake si tatava, titina, kaka sawalay. si kaka 'abayane.
S.PHON: kakə si tatava, titina, kaka sawalaj. si kaka ʔabajanə.
S.TRANSL: 我跟爸爸、媽媽、哥哥和姊姊住在一起。
I live with my father, mother, brother and sister.

S.FORM: tatavinakowaka ma'aagae. tatavi nakoa ka kisace.
S.PHON: tatavinakowaka maʔaagaə. tatavi nakoa ka kisaʦə.
S.FORM: tatavinakowaka ma'aagae. tatavi nakoa ka kisace.
S.PHON: tatavinakowaka maʔaagaə. tatavi nakoa ka kisaʦə.
S.TRANSL: 我的爸爸很高，我的爸爸是警察。
My father is tall and he is a policeman.

S.FORM: titina kowa ka mailay titinakowa ka kwisange.
S.PHON: titina kowa ka mailaj titinakowa ka kwisaŋə.
S.FORM: titina kowa ka mailay titinakowa ka kwisange.
S.PHON: titina kowa ka mailaj titinakowa ka kwisaŋə.
S.TRANSL: 我的媽媽很瘦，我的媽媽是醫生。
My mother is thin and my mom is a doctor.

S.FORM: 'ikay name da'ane ka wakacaisisiyane kiname.
S.PHON: ʔikaj namə daʔanə ka wakaʦaisisijanə kinamə.
S.FORM: 'ikay name da'ane ka wakacaisisiyane kiname.
S.PHON: ʔikaj namə daʔanə ka wakaʦaisisijanə kinamə.
S.TRANSL: 爸爸在家會和我們說族語。
My father speaks the Rukai language with us at home.

S.FORM: hoyasemi ka'abee kiname mwa tacekecekelane.
S.PHON: hojasəmi kaʔabəə kinamə mwa taʦəkəʦəkəlanə.
S.FORM: hoyasemi ka'abee kiname mwa tacekecekelane.
S.PHON: hojasəmi kaʔabəə kinamə mwa taʦəkəʦəkəlanə.
S.TRANSL: 我們暑假都會回部落。
We will all go back to the tribe during the summer vacation.

S.FORM: kake si kaka 'angese kiname mwa bwadra na takoapange.
S.PHON: kakə si kaka ʔaŋəsə kinamə mwa bwaɖa na takoapaŋə.
S.FORM: kake si kaka 'angese kiname mwa bwadra na takoapange.
S.PHON: kakə si kaka ʔaŋəsə kinamə mwa bwaɖa na takoapaŋə.
S.TRANSL: 我和兄姊會一起抓青蛙。
My brothers and sisters and I will catch frogs together.

S.FORM: madalame kake namiya ka thakada'ane.
S.PHON: madalamə kakə namija ka θakadaʔanə.
S.FORM: madalame kake namiya ka thakada'ane.
S.PHON: madalamə kakə namija ka θakadaʔanə.
S.TRANSL: 我很喜歡我們家的人。
I like our family very much.

S.FORM: sesane ka 'angese kake ki titina 'abebebee.
S.PHON: səsanə ka ʔaŋəsə kakə ki titina ʔabəbəbəə.
S.FORM: sesane ka 'angese kake ki titina 'abebebee.
S.PHON: səsanə ka ʔaŋəsə kakə ki titina ʔabəbəbəə.
S.TRANSL: 今天我和媽媽出去玩。
I went out with my mother today.

S.FORM: viviva'ane tabelengane ka mathao, va'iyane ka ikakece si ikaapa'a, besebese ka mabalesebese.
S.PHON: vivivaʔanə tabələŋanə ka maθao, vaʔijanə ka ikakəʦə si ikaapaʔa, bəsəbəsə ka mabaləsəbəsə.
S.FORM: viviva'ane tabelengane ka mathao, va'iyane ka ikakece si ikaapa'a, besebese ka mabalesebese.
S.PHON: vivivaʔanə tabələŋanə ka maθao, vaʔijanə ka ikakəʦə si ikaapaʔa, bəsəbəsə ka mabaləsəbəsə.
S.TRANSL: 白天的天空很晴朗，天氣不冷也不熱，風很涼爽。
The sky is blue during the day, the weather is neither cold nor hot, and the wind is cool.

S.FORM: 'iyakay kiname dida'ane taswaoswaovane kwane na ming-pao, kokoli'i ka wapava'i bwatiname, sawkoa malabeabee.
S.PHON: ʔijakaj kinamə didaʔanə taswaoswaovanə kwanə na miŋ-pao, kokoliʔi ka wapavaʔi bwatinamə, sawkoa malabəabəə.
S.FORM: 'iyakay kiname dida'ane taswaoswaovane kwane na ming-pao, kokoli'i ka wapava'i bwatiname, sawkoa malabeabee.
S.PHON: ʔijakaj kinamə didaʔanə taswaoswaovanə kwanə na miŋ-pao, kokoliʔi ka wapavaʔi bwatinamə, sawkoa malabəabəə.
S.TRANSL: 我們在草地上吃著麵包，陽光灑在身上，非常溫暖。
We ate bread on the grass, the sun was shining on us, and it was very warm.

S.FORM: viva'anenga ka tahelengane ka wabaythi matileange.
S.PHON: vivaʔanəŋa ka tahələŋanə ka wabajθi matiləaŋə.
S.FORM: viva'anenga ka tahelengane ka wabaythi matileange.
S.PHON: vivaʔanəŋa ka tahələŋanə ka wabajθi matiləaŋə.
S.TRANSL: 下午，天空變紅了。
In the afternoon, the sky turned red.

S.FORM: kyaboboo kake titinane, akwani tabelengane patasini ka 'atadileleangini?
S.PHON: kjaboboo kakə titinanə, akwani tabələŋanə patasini ka ʔatadilələaŋini?
S.FORM: kyaboboo kake titinane, akwani tabelengane patasini ka 'atadileleangini?
S.PHON: kjaboboo kakə titinanə, akwani tabələŋanə patasini ka ʔatadilələaŋini?
S.TRANSL: 我問媽媽，為甚麼天空是紅色的。
I asked my mother why the sky is red.

S.FORM: titina ka 'apobaka, kadromane ka no sebee ami.
S.PHON: titina ka ʔapobaka, kaɖomanə ka no səbəə ami.
S.FORM: titina ka 'apobaka, kadromane ka no sebee ami.
S.PHON: titina ka ʔapobaka, kaɖomanə ka no səbəə ami.
S.TRANSL: 媽媽說，可能是颱風要來了。
Mom said, maybe the typhoon is coming.

S.FORM: ma'awbo tabelengane ka kadroa na koongo, 'abwale na taiyaw.
S.PHON: maʔawbo tabələŋanə ka kaɖoa na kooŋo, ʔabwalə na taijaw.
S.FORM: ma'awbo tabelengane ka kadroa na koongo, 'abwale na taiyaw.
S.PHON: maʔawbo tabələŋanə ka kaɖoa na kooŋo, ʔabwalə na taijaw.
S.TRANSL: 晚上天上沒有雲，星星出來了。
There were no clouds in the sky at night, and the stars came out.

S.FORM: na 'abeenga mikiname.
S.PHON: na ʔabəəŋa mikinamə.
S.FORM: na 'abeenga mikiname.
S.PHON: na ʔabəəŋa mikinamə.
S.TRANSL: 我們也要回家了。
We were going home too.

S.FORM: 'abeeli dadaane ka wasokobo kake tabelengane.
S.PHON: ʔabəəli dadaanə ka wasokobo kakə tabələŋanə.
S.FORM: 'abeeli dadaane ka wasokobo kake tabelengane.
S.PHON: ʔabəəli dadaanə ka wasokobo kakə tabələŋanə.
S.TRANSL: 在回家的路上，我跟上天許願。
On the way home, I made a wish with the stars,

S.FORM: saamanga si i'awkay na sesbee, 'api 'abebebee la kake.
S.PHON: saamaŋa si iʔawkaj na səsbəə, ʔapi ʔabəbəbəə la kakə.
S.FORM: saamanga si i'awkay na sesbee, 'api 'abebebee la kake.
S.PHON: saamaŋa si iʔawkaj na səsbəə, ʔapi ʔabəbəbəə la kakə.
S.TRANSL: 希望颱風不要來，我還想再出去玩。
I hope the typhoon doesn't come, I still want to go out to play again.

S.FORM: gakoili ka 'iyakay da'aniname cigi'ane, pava'iyane kake dwavadavace mwa gako, si dwavadavace sivii.
S.PHON: gakoili ka ʔijakaj daʔaninamə ʦigiʔanə, pavaʔijanə kakə dwavadavaʦə mwa gako, si dwavadavaʦə sivii.
S.FORM: gakoili ka 'iyakay da'aniname cigi'ane, pava'iyane kake dwavadavace mwa gako, si dwavadavace sivii.
S.PHON: gakoili ka ʔijakaj daʔaninamə ʦigiʔanə, pavaʔijanə kakə dwavadavaʦə mwa gako, si dwavadavaʦə sivii.
S.TRANSL: 我的學校在我們家旁邊，我天天走路去學校，也走路回家。
My school is next to our house, I walk to school every day, and I also walk home.

S.FORM: isakwadraw gakoname, ikadraw na sito, maygoigo'ane maesale.
S.PHON: isakwaɖaw gakonamə, ikaɖaw na sito, majgoigoʔanə maəsalə.
S.FORM: isakwadraw gakoname, ikadraw na sito, maygoigo'ane maesale.
S.PHON: isakwaɖaw gakonamə, ikaɖaw na sito, majgoigoʔanə maəsalə.
S.TRANSL: 我們學校不大，沒有很多學生，大家互相認識。
Our school is not big, there are not many students, everyone knows each other.

S.FORM: sana 'avalavalake ka 'iyaobo 'ikay 'ongdozio, ta'aese 'angi na malri, ta'aese 'avavane.
S.PHON: sana ʔavalavalakə ka ʔijaobo ʔikaj ʔoŋdozio, taʔaəsə ʔaŋi na maɭi, taʔaəsə ʔavavanə.
S.FORM: sana 'avalavalake ka 'iyaobo 'ikay 'ongdozio, ta'aese 'angi na malri, ta'aese 'avavane.
S.PHON: sana ʔavalavalakə ka ʔijaobo ʔikaj ʔoŋdozio, taʔaəsə ʔaŋi na maɭi, taʔaəsə ʔavavanə.
S.TRANSL: 所有小朋友會聚在操場，一起玩球、一起聊天。
All the children will gather in the playground, play ball and chat together.

S.FORM: ki singsi ka ta'aese namiya 'angi, gako ka amiya na tawmomo na thakada'ane.
S.PHON: ki siŋsi ka taʔaəsə namija ʔaŋi, gako ka amija na tawmomo na θakadaʔanə.
S.FORM: ki singsi ka ta'aese namiya 'angi, gako ka amiya na tawmomo na thakada'ane.
S.PHON: ki siŋsi ka taʔaəsə namija ʔaŋi, gako ka amija na tawmomo na θakadaʔanə.
S.TRANSL: 老師們也會和我們一起玩，學校是個大家庭。
The teachers also play with us, the school is one big family.

S.FORM: pava'iyane kiname sozi, pabola'a nakay tasolasolatane.
S.PHON: pavaʔijanə kinamə sozi, pabolaʔa nakaj tasolasolatanə.
S.FORM: pava'iyane kiname sozi, pabola'a nakay tasolasolatane.
S.PHON: pavaʔijanə kinamə sozi, pabolaʔa nakaj tasolasolatanə.
S.TRANSL: 我們每天都要打掃，大家要把教室打掃乾淨。
We have to clean every day, and everyone has to clean the classroom.

S.FORM: 'iyakay na coaw wadrisidrisi na ta'avangane, 'iyakay na coaw waswape.
S.PHON: ʔijakaj na ʦoaw waɖisiɖisi na taʔavaŋanə, ʔijakaj na ʦoaw waswapə.
S.FORM: 'iyakay na coaw wadrisidrisi na ta'avangane, 'iyakay na coaw waswape.
S.PHON: ʔijakaj na ʦoaw waɖisiɖisi na taʔavaŋanə, ʔijakaj na ʦoaw waswapə.
S.TRANSL: 有些人去擦窗戶，有些人去掃地。
Some people clean the windows, some sweep the floor.

S.FORM: waswape kiname gako ma'akaynoinonga, matibocobocongo kiname, gako ka pava'iyane mabola'a.
S.PHON: waswapə kinamə gako maʔakajnoinoŋa, matiboʦoboʦoŋo kinamə, gako ka pavaʔijanə mabolaʔa.
S.FORM: waswape kiname gako ma'akaynoinonga, matibocobocongo kiname, gako ka pava'iyane mabola'a.
S.PHON: waswapə kinamə gako maʔakajnoinoŋa, matiboʦoboʦoŋo kinamə, gako ka pavaʔijanə mabolaʔa.
S.TRANSL: 我們還會打掃學校的各個角落，大家分工合作，學校每天都很乾淨。
We will also clean every corner of the school. Everyone works together, and the school is very clean every day.

S.FORM: kosi'a ka, amwa kake tecekecekelane swapepeale twamane.
S.PHON: kosiʔa ka, amwa kakə təʦəkəʦəkəlanə swapəpəalə twamanə.

S.PHON: kosiʔa ka, amwa kakə təʦəkəʦəkəlanə swapəpəalə twamanə.
S.TRANSL: 昨天，我去部落拜訪爺爺。
Yesterday, I went to visit my grandpa in the tribe.

S.FORM: 'iyakikaynga kiname ipacengecengele, kyadredreme kake nyane.
S.PHON: ʔijakikajŋa kinamə ipaʦəŋəʦəŋələ, kjaɖəɖəmə kakə njanə.

S.PHON: ʔijakikajŋa kinamə ipaʦəŋəʦəŋələ, kjaɖəɖəmə kakə njanə.
S.TRANSL: 我們很久沒有見面了，我很想念他。
We haven't seen each other for a long time and I miss him a lot.

S.FORM: tawmo ko cengelini koa ka pakane nakoa na si-kwa, la pa'ongolo nakoa na 'ocya.
S.PHON: tawmo ko ʦəŋəlini koa ka pakanə nakoa na si-kwa, la paʔoŋolo nakoa na ʔoʦja.

S.TRANSL: 爺爺一看到我，就請我吃西瓜，請我喝茶。
As soon as grandpa saw me, he invited me to eat watermelon and drink tea.

S.FORM: doko'ini ki tawmo ko si-kwa ka malimeme sawkoa, maagi'i ko kwanimade.
S.PHON: dokoʔini ki tawmo ko si-kwa ka maliməmə sawkoa, maagiʔi ko kwanimadə.

S.PHON: dokoʔini ki tawmo ko si-kwa ka maliməmə sawkoa, maagiʔi ko kwanimadə.
S.TRANSL: 爺爺種的西瓜很甜，真的很好吃。
The watermelon grown by grandpa is very sweet and really delicious.

S.FORM: madalame kake 'ocyaini ki tawmo, maagi'i ko 'ongolimade.
S.PHON: madalamə kakə ʔoʦjaini ki tawmo, maagiʔi ko ʔoŋolimadə.

S.PHON: madalamə kakə ʔoʦjaini ki tawmo, maagiʔi ko ʔoŋolimadə.
S.TRANSL: 我也喜歡爺爺的茶，也很好喝。
I also like grandpa's tea, it's tasted good too.

S.FORM: viva'ane ka takadrosa kiname ki takawngo do'o na kidrali.
S.PHON: vivaʔanə ka takaɖosa kinamə ki takawŋo doʔo na kiɖali.

S.PHON: vivaʔanə ka takaɖosa kinamə ki takawŋo doʔo na kiɖali.
S.TRANSL: 中午的時候，我和奶奶一起煮午餐。
At noon, I cooked lunch with my grandma.

S.FORM: takawngo ka tyakanekane maagi'i na kidrali, 'iyakay na ka'ange 'iyakay na tawkoko 'iyakay na tangetange, la 'iyakay laana tawlolo.
S.PHON: takawŋo ka tjakanəkanə maagiʔi na kiɖali, ʔijakaj na kaʔaŋə ʔijakaj na tawkoko ʔijakaj na taŋətaŋə, la ʔijakaj laana tawlolo.

S.TRANSL: 奶奶煮了很豐盛的午餐，有魚、有雞、有南瓜、還有蝸牛。
Grandma cooked a big lunch, including fish, chicken, pumpkin, and snails.

S.FORM: makatikidraliname, lawa kiname talacengane kilacenge.
S.PHON: makatikiɖalinamə, lawa kinamə talaʦəŋanə kilaʦəŋə.

S.PHON: makatikiɖalinamə, lawa kinamə talaʦəŋanə kilaʦəŋə.
S.TRANSL: 吃完午餐後，我們去菜園摘菜。
After lunch, we went to the vegetable garden to pick vegetables.

S.FORM: takawngo ka amalra 'angiyadraw na bwathi, tay si tangavane bwa'ay koa, wameca 'abee mya.
S.PHON: takawŋo ka amaɭa ʔaŋijaɖaw na bwaθi, taj si taŋavanə bwaʔaj koa, waməʦa ʔabəə mja.

S.TRANSL: 奶奶拿了很多地瓜、芋頭和蔥給我，要我帶回家。
Grandma brought me a lot of sweet potatoes, taro and buckwheat, and asked me to take them home.

S.FORM: amwa kake nyane posao, 'awkay lami kake cengele mwane.
S.PHON: amwa kakə njanə posao, ʔawkaj lami kakə ʦəŋələ mwanə.

S.TRANSL: 我跟他們道別，說下次再去看他們。
I said goodbye to them and said I'd see them next time.

S.FORM: sesane ka wameamece namiya ki singsi mwa tong-'o-ying, la 'ikay kiname balingtavane matilibi lwangay na kipe.
S.PHON: səsanə ka waməaməʦə namija ki siŋsi mwa toŋ-ʔo-jiŋ, la ʔikaj kinamə baliŋtavanə matilibi lwaŋaj na kipə.

S.TRANSL: 今天老師帶我們去動物園，我們在門口排隊買票。
Today the teacher took us to the zoo, and we lined up at the gate to buy tickets.

S.FORM: moda'aniname tong-'o-ying ka, la 'apobaka ki singsi, "talocobongane ka alralrame, ikodrangane ka swa'a, mo'angekidredrema komo mwa cengecengele."

S.TRANSL: so you can visit them yourself.""
After entering the zoo, the teacher said, There are birds in the front and snakes in the back

S.FORM: 'akolodro kake na swa'a, nikwadra icengele kake na swa'a.
S.PHON: ʔakoloɖo kakə na swaʔa, nikwaɖa iʦəŋələ kakə na swaʔa.

S.TRANSL: 我會怕蛇，所以我不看蛇類。
I'm afraid of snakes, so I don't look at snakes.

S.FORM: wacengele kake 'angiyadraw na alralrame, 'iyakay 'angato agae 'angopalapalay, la cengele laa kokongo 'ikay ta'angatongatoane na mawbong 'adringi syaeke.
S.PHON: waʦəŋələ kakə ʔaŋijaɖaw na aɭaɭamə, ʔijakaj ʔaŋato agaə ʔaŋopalapalaj, la ʦəŋələ laa kokoŋo ʔikaj taʔaŋatoŋatoanə na mawboŋ ʔaɖiŋi sjaəkə.

S.TRANSL: 我看到了很多鳥，他們在樹上飛來飛去，還有看到貓頭鷹在樹洞裡睡覺。
I saw a lot of birds flying around in the trees and saw owls sleeping in tree hollows.

S.FORM: tong-'o-ying 'adringi ka 'iyakay na zo, 'ema si ikolaw, icengelela kake ami nakay na golagolay, sesane ka mabeabee kake nakay tacengelanili.

S.TRANSL: 動物園裡有大象、馬和老虎，我從前沒有看過這些動物，今天看到了真的很開心。
There are elephants, horses and tigers in the zoo. I have never seen these animals before. I was really happy to see them today.

S.FORM: madraw ko pakisyasingili, pacengele tatava si titina.
S.PHON: maɖaw ko pakisjasiŋili, paʦəŋələ tatava si titina.

S.TRANSL: 我拍了很多照片，給爸爸媽媽看。
I took a lot of pictures and showed them to my parents.

S.FORM: bilanenga ka no 'angese mikiname tatava si titina 'awkay.

S.TRANSL: 下次要跟爸爸媽媽一起來。
Next time I will come with my parents.

S.FORM: kikay ta'isasadrane va'iyane ka, kake si kaka 'abayane ka amwa Taito swapepeale tatavane.

S.TRANSL: 這個假日，我和姊姊去台東拜訪叔叔。
This holiday, my sister and I went to Taitung to visit my uncle.

S.FORM: syalikay kiname Taihoko 'angokisya mwa Taito.
S.PHON: sjalikaj kinamə taihoko ʔaŋokisja mwa taito.

S.TRANSL: 我們從台北搭火車去臺東。
We took the train from Taipei to Taitung.

S.FORM: mwaninga Taito, 'angosengepae 'awkay tisyaba dresenge namiya ki tatava.
S.PHON: mwaniŋa taito, ʔaŋosəŋəpaə ʔawkaj tisjaba ɖəsəŋə namija ki tatava.

S.PHON: mwaniŋa taito, ʔaŋosəŋəpaə ʔawkaj tisjaba ɖəsəŋə namija ki tatava.
S.TRANSL: 到了台東，叔叔開車來車站接我們。
When we arrived in Taitung, my uncle drove to the station to pick us up.

S.FORM: tisyaba ka ika'adavili da'anini ki tatava ki 'Ase, 'angesangepaemade ka wadodao'o kimade mwa.
S.PHON: tisjaba ka ikaʔadavili daʔanini ki tatava ki ʔasə, ʔaŋəsaŋəpaəmadə ka wadodaoʔo kimadə mwa.

S.TRANSL: 車站離’Ase叔叔家沒有很遠，開車一下子就到了。
The station is not far from my uncle's house, and it's just a short drive away.

S.FORM: tatava 'Ase da'anini ka wadali tathadethade'ane, 'angeese kiname 'angotawngatingi mwa tathadethade'ane.
S.PHON: tatava ʔasə daʔanini ka wadali taθadəθadəʔanə, ʔaŋəəsə kinamə ʔaŋotawŋatiŋi mwa taθadəθadəʔanə.

S.TRANSL: ’Ase叔叔家附近有個很大的牧場，我們一起騎腳踏車到牧場。
There is a big ranch near my uncle's house, and we rode bicycles to the ranch together.

S.FORM: 'ikay tathadethade'ane, tatava 'Ase wameamece namiya pakane na saw'iyo si 'ango'ema.
S.PHON: ʔikaj taθadəθadəʔanə, tatava ʔasə waməaməʦə namija pakanə na sawʔijo si ʔaŋoʔəma.

S.TRANSL: 在牧場，’Ase叔叔帶我們餵羊和騎馬。
In the ranch, my uncle took us to feed the sheep and ride horses.

S.FORM: ma'awbonga ka, la 'angese kiname mwa tokosi kwane na do'o.
S.PHON: maʔawboŋa ka, la ʔaŋəsə kinamə mwa tokosi kwanə na doʔo.
S.FORM: ma'awbonga ka, la 'angese kiname mwa tokosi kwane na do'o.
S.PHON: maʔawboŋa ka, la ʔaŋəsə kinamə mwa tokosi kwanə na doʔo.
S.TRANSL: 晚上，我們一起去市區吃飯。
In the evening, we went to the city to have dinner together.

S.FORM: tokosi ka madraw na sangepae, nikwaname i'ango'otobay mwa drayne.
S.PHON: tokosi ka maɖaw na saŋəpaə, nikwanamə iʔaŋoʔotobaj mwa ɖajnə.

S.PHON: tokosi ka maɖaw na saŋəpaə, nikwanamə iʔaŋoʔotobaj mwa ɖajnə.
S.TRANSL: 市區車子很多，所以我們騎摩托車去那裡。
There are a lot of cars in the city, so we go there by motorcycle.

S.FORM: ta'avagay myaname ka, laso igo'o kiname kya'opa na coaw 'otobay swaongaynga kiname 'angobase 'avagay.
S.PHON: taʔavagaj mjanamə ka, laso igoʔo kinamə kjaʔopa na ʦoaw ʔotobaj swaoŋajŋa kinamə ʔaŋobasə ʔavagaj.

S.TRANSL: 要離開的時候，發現摩托車被偷了，我們只好搭公車離開。
When we were about to leave, we found that the motorcycle had been stolen, so we had to leave by bus.

S.FORM: nakay ka 'abeenga kiname mwa Taihoko, amiyani ko 'otobayni ki tatava 'Ase ka mwakononga nyani.
S.PHON: nakaj ka ʔabəəŋa kinamə mwa taihoko, amijani ko ʔotobajni ki tatava ʔasə ka mwakonoŋa njani.

S.TRANSL: 現在我們回到台北了，不知道’Ase叔叔的摩托車找到了沒有。
Now that we are back in Taipei, I don't know if my uncle's motorcycle has been found.

S.FORM: ko 'avalavalakili ka 'iyakay kiname Kongadavane, ko patekedriname ka la 'aela kiname mwa Takaw sikace.
S.PHON: ko ʔavalavalakili ka ʔijakaj kinamə koŋadavanə, ko patəkəɖinamə ka la ʔaəla kinamə mwa takaw sikaʦə.
S.FORM: ko 'avalavalakili ka 'iyakay kiname Kongadavane, ko patekedriname ka la 'aela kiname mwa Takaw sikace.
S.PHON: ko ʔavalavalakili ka ʔijakaj kinamə koŋadavanə, ko patəkəɖinamə ka la ʔaəla kinamə mwa takaw sikaʦə.
S.TRANSL: 小時候我們在多納，但是長大就搬到了高雄，在都市裡生活。
When we were young, our family lived in Kongadavane. After I started school, we moved to Kaohsiung and have been living in the city ever since.

S.FORM: ko mwalinga daigake ka la kica'oane kake na tolwanini na kacaisyane, lasoigo'o kake Taiwan kacaisyane na ma'ielela na bengka.
S.PHON: ko mwaliŋa daigakə ka la kiʦaʔoanə kakə na tolwanini na kaʦaisjanə, lasoigoʔo kakə taiwan kaʦaisjanə na maʔiələla na bəŋka.
S.FORM: ko mwalinga daigake ka la kica'oane kake na tolwanini na kacaisyane, lasoigo'o kake Taiwan kacaisyane na ma'ielela na bengka.
S.PHON: ko mwaliŋa daigakə ka la kiʦaʔoanə kakə na tolwanini na kaʦaisjanə, lasoigoʔo kakə taiwan kaʦaisjanə na maʔiələla na bəŋka.
S.TRANSL: 上了大學以後，我修了一堂關於原住民文化的課程，瞭解到臺灣原住民族有各式各樣豐富的文化。
After I went to college, I took a course on aboriginal culture and learned that the aboriginal people in Taiwan have various and rich cultures.

S.FORM: nikwani nakay cicavili hoyasemi kake si kaka la 'abee kiname mwa tacekecekelane kiboboo na maodrange. taigo'o na bengkaname mya.
S.PHON: nikwani nakaj ʦiʦavili hojasəmi kakə si kaka la ʔabəə kinamə mwa taʦəkəʦəkəlanə kiboboo na maoɖaŋə. taigoʔo na bəŋkanamə mja.
S.FORM: nikwani nakay cicavili hoyasemi kake si kaka la 'abee kiname mwa tacekecekelane kiboboo na maodrange. taigo'o na bengkaname mya.
S.PHON: nikwani nakaj ʦiʦavili hojasəmi kakə si kaka la ʔabəə kinamə mwa taʦəkəʦəkəlanə kiboboo na maoɖaŋə. taigoʔo na bəŋkanamə mja.
S.TRANSL: 因此，今年暑假時，我和哥哥回到部落訪問老人家，想要瞭解自己族群的文化。
So this summer, my brother and I went back to our tribe to visit the elderly, hoping to understand the culture of our own ethnic group.

S.FORM: maodrange ka watamavaolonga na cavilini, makasi cengelimade ka amiya na 'athabaela.
S.PHON: maoɖaŋə ka watamavaoloŋa na ʦavilini, makasi ʦəŋəlimadə ka amija na ʔaθabaəla.
S.FORM: maodrange ka watamavaolonga na cavilini, makasi cengelimade ka amiya na 'athabaela.
S.PHON: maoɖaŋə ka watamavaoloŋa na ʦavilini, makasi ʦəŋəlimadə ka amija na ʔaθabaəla.
S.TRANSL: 老人家已經八十歲了，但是看起來還像中年人。
The old man is already eighty years old, but he still looks like a middle-aged man.

S.FORM: la kiboboo kake nyane maniigo'iso na baka mya, maygili 'apobaka na wadrekadrekane, waigo'o na wapakikisane. wakokaomane si wa'amolrilrikane.
S.PHON: la kiboboo kakə njanə maniigoʔiso na baka mja, majgili ʔapobaka na waɖəkaɖəkanə, waigoʔo na wapakikisanə. wakokaomanə si waʔamoɭiɭikanə.
S.FORM: la kiboboo kake nyane maniigo'iso na baka mya, maygili 'apobaka na wadrekadrekane, waigo'o na wapakikisane. wakokaomane si wa'amolrilrikane.
S.PHON: la kiboboo kakə njanə maniigoʔiso na baka mja, majgili ʔapobaka na waɖəkaɖəkanə, waigoʔo na wapakikisanə. wakokaomanə si waʔamoɭiɭikanə.
S.TRANSL: 我問他會哪些語言，他的魯凱語說得很流利，也會說閩南語、日語、英語。
I asked him which languages he knew, and he was fluent in Rukai, as well as Hokkien, Japanese and English.

S.FORM: ko boboilinga lasoigo'o kake ko 'avalavalakini ka kyaca'oane na wakokaomane, palangini ka pakisa, si ta'akomolanini ka la paygoigo'ane na taligaykoko, nikwani igo'o 'angiyadraw na ma'akabakanga.
S.PHON: ko boboiliŋa lasoigoʔo kakə ko ʔavalavalakini ka kjaʦaʔoanə na wakokaomanə, palaŋini ka pakisa, si taʔakomolanini ka la pajgoigoʔanə na taligajkoko, nikwani igoʔo ʔaŋijaɖaw na maʔakabakaŋa.
S.FORM: ko boboilinga lasoigo'o kake ko 'avalavalakini ka kyaca'oane na wakokaomane, palangini ka pakisa, si ta'akomolanini ka la paygoigo'ane na taligaykoko, nikwani igo'o 'angiyadraw na ma'akabakanga.
S.PHON: ko boboiliŋa lasoigoʔo kakə ko ʔavalavalakini ka kjaʦaʔoanə na wakokaomanə, palaŋini ka pakisa, si taʔakomolanini ka la pajgoigoʔanə na taligajkoko, nikwani igoʔo ʔaŋijaɖaw na maʔakabakaŋa.
S.TRANSL: 我問他才知道，原來他小時候就學日本話，太太是漢人，而他工作上認識許多外國人，所以他會說這麼多語言。
I asked him to find out that he learned Japanese when he was a child, his wife is an Chinese, and he knows many foreign at work, so he can speak so many languages.

S.FORM: ko mabakabakainame ka lapatolotolnga kiname taiyanini na walopo.
S.PHON: ko mabakabakainamə ka lapatolotolŋa kinamə taijanini na walopo.
S.FORM: ko mabakabakainame ka lapatolotolnga kiname taiyanini na walopo.
S.PHON: ko mabakabakainamə ka lapatolotolŋa kinamə taijanini na walopo.
S.TRANSL: 我們訪談的時候，老人家談論了許多關於狩獵的傳統知識。
During our interview, the old man talked about a lot of traditional knowledge about hunting.

S.FORM: itakaigo'ane kiname bicingi, makasi syakay kiname na so-ci lo-ing, la doo la kiname pa'ingcege biciingi.
S.PHON: itakaigoʔanə kinamə biʦiŋi, makasi sjakaj kinamə na so-ʦi lo-iŋ, la doo la kinamə paʔiŋʦəgə biʦiiŋi.
S.FORM: itakaigo'ane kiname bicingi, makasi syakay kiname na so-ci lo-ing, la doo la kiname pa'ingcege biciingi.
S.PHON: itakaigoʔanə kinamə biʦiŋi, makasi sjakaj kinamə na so-ʦi lo-iŋ, la doo la kinamə paʔiŋʦəgə biʦiiŋi.
S.TRANSL: 雖然我們沒有辦法完全聽懂，但是我們有用手機錄影，可以再仔細聆聽。
Although we could't fully understand it, we could record it on our cell phones and listen to it more.

S.FORM: 'avalavalakili ka itakaigo'ane kake na bengka kacaisyane, 'angi ka'ino kake taakacaisyanili.
S.PHON: ʔavalavalakili ka itakaigoʔanə kakə na bəŋka kaʦaisjanə, ʔaŋi kaʔino kakə taakaʦaisjanili.
S.FORM: 'avalavalakili ka itakaigo'ane kake na bengka kacaisyane, 'angi ka'ino kake taakacaisyanili.
S.PHON: ʔavalavalakili ka itakaigoʔanə kakə na bəŋka kaʦaisjanə, ʔaŋi kaʔino kakə taakaʦaisjanili.
S.TRANSL: 小時候我不瞭解自己族群的文化，對於自己是原住民有點不好意思。
When I was young, I didn't understand the culture of my ethnic group, and I was a little embarrassed that I was an aborigine.

S.FORM: madrawininga igo'ili la kabeabeenga kake, 'angipakileve thakongadavane kake mya.
S.PHON: maɖawiniŋa igoʔili la kabəabəəŋa kakə, ʔaŋipakiləvə θakoŋadavanə kakə mja.

S.PHON: maɖawiniŋa igoʔili la kabəabəəŋa kakə, ʔaŋipakiləvə θakoŋadavanə kakə mja.
S.TRANSL: 瞭解愈多以後，愈覺得自己身為多納人，是很獨特的，也很值得驕傲。
The more I know, the more I feel that being a Kongadavane is unique and something to be proud of.

S.FORM: sesane masaemesemini ka 'abee ami kigili mwa da'ane.
S.PHON: səsanə masaəməsəmini ka ʔabəə ami kigili mwa daʔanə.

S.PHON: səsanə masaəməsəmini ka ʔabəə ami kigili mwa daʔanə.
S.TRANSL: 今天傍晚，弟弟就要回家了。
My younger brother is going home this evening.

S.FORM: kake si takawngo ka wamikiname talangalangaane lwangay na tawkoko. paagele do'o na ki'awbo pakane giliya.
S.PHON: kakə si takawŋo ka wamikinamə talaŋalaŋaanə lwaŋaj na tawkoko. paagələ doʔo na kiʔawbo pakanə gilija.

S.TRANSL: 我和奶奶去市場買雞，準備煮好吃的晚餐給弟弟。
My grandma and I went to the market to buy chickens and prepared a delicious dinner for my younger brother.

S.FORM: waibate kiname talangalangaane na lacenge, madraw na maagi'i na lacenge.
S.PHON: waibatə kinamə talaŋalaŋaanə na laʦəŋə, maɖaw na maagiʔi na laʦəŋə.

S.TRANSL: 我們經過了菜攤，有好多新鮮的青菜。
We passed the vegetable stand, which had a lot of fresh green vegetables.

S.FORM: lalangay kiname na 'ingcay toba'a si ningzingi.
S.PHON: lalaŋaj kinamə na ʔiŋʦaj tobaʔa si niŋziŋi.
S.FORM: lalangay kiname na 'ingcay toba'a si ningzingi.
S.PHON: lalaŋaj kinamə na ʔiŋʦaj tobaʔa si niŋziŋi.
S.TRANSL: 我們買了空心菜、山藥和胡蘿蔔。
We bought water spinach, yams and carrots.

S.FORM: cigi'ane walangalangay na madro lawpange ka la'apobaka, "pangodralili si somomo ka malimeme. lwangaa 'angititi'ici."
S.PHON: ʦigiʔanə walaŋalaŋaj na maɖo lawpaŋə ka laʔapobaka, "paŋoɖalili si somomo ka maliməmə. lwaŋaa ʔaŋititiʔiʦi."

S.TRANSL: 旁邊水果攤的老闆說:＂我的鳳梨和桃子很甜。買一些吧!＂
The owner of the nearby fruit stand said: "My pineapples and peaches are very sweet. Buy some!"

S.FORM: walangay kiname na pangodrale si somomo.
S.PHON: walaŋaj kinamə na paŋoɖalə si somomo.
S.FORM: walangay kiname na pangodrale si somomo.
S.PHON: walaŋaj kinamə na paŋoɖalə si somomo.
S.TRANSL: 我們買了鳳梨和桃子。
We bought pineapples and peaches.

S.FORM: laibate kiname talangalangaane na bwate, lawpange la'apobaka, "mani langayaniso? sesane na tawkoko ka tawmomo mamitee, sesesane ka too na idai na payso.

S.FORM: laibate kiname talangalangaane na bwate, lawpange la'apobaka, "mani langayaniso? sesane na tawkoko ka tawmomo mamitee, sesesane ka too na idai na payso.

S.TRANSL: 我們經過了肉攤，老闆說:＂你要買什麼?今天的雞又大又肥，一隻300元。＂
We passed the meat stall, and the boss said, "What do you want to buy? Today's chickens are big and fat, and one is 300 NTD."

S.FORM: takawngo ka makocingay laya, lakiboboo lawpangane. doo myaso tipadodoo 'angititi'ici.
S.PHON: takawŋo ka makoʦiŋaj laja, lakiboboo lawpaŋanə. doo mjaso tipadodoo ʔaŋititiʔiʦi.

S.TRANSL: 奶奶覺得太貴了，問老闆可不可以便宜一點。
Grandma thought it was too expensive and asked the boss if it could be cheaper.

S.FORM: makasi lawpange ka la'apobaka, "mapadodoonga, dooiyanga oalralra."
S.PHON: makasi lawpaŋə ka laʔapobaka, "mapadodooŋa, dooijaŋa oaɭaɭa."

S.TRANSL: 但老闆說已經很便宜，不能再減了。
But the boss said that it is already very cheap and cannot be reduced any further.

S.FORM: cigi'ane lawpange la'apobaka, "ngoalra lwangaa ka'angili, ka'angili ka tawmomo si lakapadodoo."
S.PHON: ʦigiʔanə lawpaŋə laʔapobaka, "ŋoaɭa lwaŋaa kaʔaŋili, kaʔaŋili ka tawmomo si lakapadodoo."

S.TRANSL: 隔壁的老闆說：＂來買我的魚，我的魚又大又便宜。＂
The boss next door said, come and buy my fish, my fish is big and cheap.

S.FORM: biilanenga lalangay kiname na tawkoko si ka'ange, la'abee kiname do'o pasiéte cekei.
S.PHON: biilanəŋa lalaŋaj kinamə na tawkoko si kaʔaŋə, laʔabəə kinamə doʔo pasi*tə ʦəkəi.

S.TRANSL: 最後我們買了雞也買了魚，回家煮了一桌子的菜。
In the end we bought chicken and fish, and went home to cook a table of food.

S.FORM: la'ika'ikay kiname 'oadra'adraw. lakasongonga.
S.PHON: laʔikaʔikaj kinamə ʔoaɖaʔaɖaw. lakasoŋoŋa.

S.TRANSL: 等了好久，天已經黑了。
After waiting for a long time, it was already dark.

S.FORM: la dingva ki gili pa'abee, betai ka i'isasadra, sakasokobanenga ka doo mikake 'abee mya.
S.PHON: la diŋva ki gili paʔabəə, bətai ka iʔisasaɖa, sakasokobanəŋa ka doo mikakə ʔabəə mja.

S.TRANSL: 弟弟打電話回來說，軍隊突然不能放假了，要下一個禮拜日才回來。
The younger brother called back and said that the army suddenly couldn't take a vacation and would not be back until the next Sunday.

S.FORM: takawngo ka la'apobaka do'onga 'akathay nakay, kokwava tatakaese kwane.
S.PHON: takawŋo ka laʔapobaka doʔoŋa ʔakaθaj nakaj, kokwava tatakaəsə kwanə.

S.TRANSL: 奶奶說已經煮了這麼多，叫大家一起來吃吧!
Grandma said that she has cooked so much, so let's eat together!

S.FORM: la'alralra kiname talinamiyanane takaese tiki'awbo 'angitaleke.
S.PHON: laʔaɭaɭa kinamə talinamijananə takaəsə tikiʔawbo ʔaŋitaləkə.

S.TRANSL: 我們邀請了親朋好友，一起來享用豐盛的晚餐。
We invited friends and family to have a great dinner together.

S.FORM: 'iyakay 'asaseane na soi'anili acini ka 'Aase, 'ataleese maimoo wamece na ma'akoanga.
S.PHON: ʔijakaj ʔasasəanə na soiʔanili aʦini ka ʔaasə, ʔataləəsə maimoo waməʦə na maʔakoaŋa.

S.TRANSL: 我有一個同學叫’Aase，他常常忘記帶東西。
I have a classmate named 'Aase, he often forgets to bring things.

S.FORM: solatiname ka lakisodame soi'aniname na 'ingpice, sadrisidrisi si ciwmingi.
S.PHON: solatinamə ka lakisodamə soiʔaninamə na ʔiŋpiʦə, saɖisiɖisi si ʦiwmiŋi.

S.TRANSL: 上課的時候，他跟同學借鉛筆、橡皮擦或是筆記本。
During class, he borrowed pencils, erasers or notebooks from his classmates.

S.FORM: kovasale, koposale, kotoolo maesale doo pasodamenyane.
S.PHON: kovasalə, koposalə, kotoolo maəsalə doo pasodamənjanə.

S.TRANSL: 一次、兩次、三次，大家會借東西給他。
Once, twice, three times, everyone would lend him something.

S.FORM: makasi, 'iyakay na makoace ka sakaini 'ataleese kisodame na ma'akakoanga si isavivii.

S.TRANSL: 但是他有個壞習慣，經常借了東西卻不還。
But he has a bad habit of often borrowing things but not returning them.

S.FORM: biilanenga lakesale masamadre ipasodamenganyane.
S.PHON: biilanəŋa lakəsalə masamaɖə ipasodaməŋanjanə.

S.PHON: biilanəŋa lakəsalə masamaɖə ipasodaməŋanjanə.
S.TRANSL: 最後大家都不想借他東西了。
In the end, no one wanted to borrow anything from him.

S.FORM: la'ikay ko va'iyane, la'angeese kiname 'abebebee.
S.PHON: laʔikaj ko vaʔijanə, laʔaŋəəsə kinamə ʔabəbəbəə.

S.TRANSL: 有一天，我們大家一起去郊遊。
One day, we all went on an outing together.

S.FORM: ikainame dadaane, 'Aase la'apiyowa bingzyo makasi lakaimoo na wisengce.
S.PHON: ikainamə dadaanə, ʔaasə laʔapijowa biŋzjo makasi lakaimoo na wisəŋʦə.
S.FORM: ikainame dadaane, 'Aase la'apiyowa bingzyo makasi lakaimoo na wisengce.

S.TRANSL: 路途中，’Aase想上廁所，但他忘記帶衛生紙。
On the way, 'Aase wanted to go to the bathroom, but he forgot to bring toilet paper.

S.FORM: kosisodame na wisengce laya soi'anini, makasi lakaesele 'akobii, iamece kinamemya.
S.PHON: kosisodamə na wisəŋʦə laja soiʔanini, makasi lakaəsələ ʔakobii, iaməʦə kinaməmja.
S.FORM: kosisodame na wisengce laya soi'anini, makasi lakaesele 'akobii, iamece kinamemya.

S.TRANSL: 他跟同學借衛生紙，可是大家都回他說，我也沒有帶耶。
He borrowed toilet paper from his classmates, but everyone told him that I didn't bring them either.

S.FORM: 'Aase ka sotobinga, lakagi'imoco, akwamo ka nimipasodame nakoa na wisengce.

S.TRANSL: ’Aase急的哭了出來。他生氣的說，為什麼大家都不借我衛生紙!
'Aase burst into tears. He said angrily, why don't everyone lend me toilet paper!

S.FORM: soi'anini ka la 'apobaka nyane, nikwani ka i'angiamece koso, no pasodamimade mosoa na ma'akakwanga koso ka isavivii.
S.PHON: soiʔanini ka la ʔapobaka njanə, nikwani ka iʔaŋiaməʦə koso, no pasodamimadə mosoa na maʔakakwaŋa koso ka isavivii.
S.FORM: soi'anini ka la 'apobaka nyane, nikwani ka i'angiamece koso, no pasodamimade mosoa na ma'akakwanga koso ka isavivii.
S.PHON: soiʔanini ka la ʔapobaka njanə, nikwani ka iʔaŋiaməʦə koso, no pasodamimadə mosoa na maʔakakwaŋa koso ka isavivii.
S.TRANSL: 同學告訴他，因為你都不自己帶，而且借給你的東西，你都不會還!
The classmate told him, because you didn't bring it yourself, and you wouldn't return the things lent to you!

S.FORM: myasi'anonga ko mwani gako, 'Aase ka la 'angelakese mwa gako. 'Aase laalra ko kisodamini na ma'akakwanga swavivii bwa'ay ko soi'anini.
S.PHON: mjasiʔanoŋa ko mwani gako, ʔaasə ka la ʔaŋəlakəsə mwa gako. ʔaasə laaɭa ko kisodamini na maʔakakwaŋa swavivii bwaʔaj ko soiʔanini.
S.FORM: myasi'anonga ko mwani gako, 'Aase ka la 'angelakese mwa gako. 'Aase laalra ko kisodamini na ma'akakwanga swavivii bwa'ay ko soi'anini.
S.PHON: mjasiʔanoŋa ko mwani gako, ʔaasə ka la ʔaŋəlakəsə mwa gako. ʔaasə laaɭa ko kisodamini na maʔakakwaŋa swavivii bwaʔaj ko soiʔanini.
S.TRANSL: 隔天上學，’Aase很早就到學校了，’Aase把跟同學借的東西都還給同學們。
The next day at school, 'Aase arrived at school early and returned everything he had borrowed from his classmates.

S.FORM: 'Aase piingavanga komo laya ko soi'anini, la 'apobaka, no biilanenga ko 'angiamece mikake na ma'akakwanga, mabeabee ko booane ladoo kibaka pasodame na ma'akakwanga.
S.PHON: ʔaasə piiŋavaŋa komo laja ko soiʔanini, la ʔapobaka, no biilanəŋa ko ʔaŋiaməʦə mikakə na maʔakakwaŋa, mabəabəə ko booanə ladoo kibaka pasodamə na maʔakakwaŋa.
S.FORM: 'Aase piingavanga komo laya ko soi'anini, la 'apobaka, no biilanenga ko 'angiamece mikake na ma'akakwanga, mabeabee ko booane ladoo kibaka pasodame na ma'akakwanga.
S.PHON: ʔaasə piiŋavaŋa komo laja ko soiʔanini, la ʔapobaka, no biilanəŋa ko ʔaŋiaməʦə mikakə na maʔakakwaŋa, mabəabəə ko booanə ladoo kibaka pasodamə na maʔakakwaŋa.
S.TRANSL: ’Aase向同學們道歉，並說他以後一定會自己帶東西，也謝謝同學們之前願意借他東西。
'Aase apologized to the students and said that he would definitely bring his own things in the future, and thanked the classmates for their willingness to lend him things before.

S.FORM: tatavi nakoa ka 'asalwalopane 'ikay tacekecekelane.
S.PHON: tatavi nakoa ka ʔasalwalopanə ʔikaj taʦəkəʦəkəlanə.
S.FORM: tatavi nakoa ka 'asalwalopane 'ikay tacekecekelane.
S.PHON: tatavi nakoa ka ʔasalwalopanə ʔikaj taʦəkəʦəkəlanə.
S.TRANSL: 我的爸爸是部落的獵人。
My dad is a hunter of the tribe.

S.FORM: 'avalavalakilika, tatava silamama no masongo ka laobalate, wamece na kwange si livase.
S.PHON: ʔavalavalakilika, tatava silamama no masoŋo ka laobalatə, waməʦə na kwaŋə si livasə.
S.FORM: 'avalavalakilika, tatava silamama no masongo ka laobalate, wamece na kwange si livase.
S.PHON: ʔavalavalakilika, tatava silamama no masoŋo ka laobalatə, waməʦə na kwaŋə si livasə.
S.TRANSL: 我小的時候，爸爸和叔叔會在晚上出門，帶著獵刀和獵槍。
When I was little, my dad and uncle would go out at night with hunting knives and guns.

S.FORM: leleapininga laameco ko alopini na bee 'abee.
S.PHON: lələapiniŋa laaməʦo ko alopini na bəə ʔabəə.
S.FORM: leleapininga laameco ko alopini na bee 'abee.
S.PHON: lələapiniŋa laaməʦo ko alopini na bəə ʔabəə.
S.TRANSL: 天亮時，他們就會帶著獵到的獵物回來。
At daybreak, they returned with their prey.

S.FORM: no kadromane ka valisane, kadromane ka ava'a, la 'ikay na 'atiko'asini.
S.PHON: no kaɖomanə ka valisanə, kaɖomanə ka avaʔa, la ʔikaj na ʔatikoʔasini.
S.FORM: no kadromane ka valisane, kadromane ka ava'a, la 'ikay na 'atiko'asini.
S.PHON: no kaɖomanə ka valisanə, kaɖomanə ka avaʔa, la ʔikaj na ʔatikoʔasini.
S.TRANSL: 有時候是山豬、有時候是飛鼠，偶爾也會有雉雞。
Sometimes it was a boar, sometimes a flying squirrel, and occasionally a pheasant.

S.FORM: kinadro ka 'iyakay tacekecekelane balate saoda'ane lwapii ko alraninabee, bway talinya nane.
S.PHON: kinaɖo ka ʔijakaj taʦəkəʦəkəlanə balatə saodaʔanə lwapii ko aɭaninabəə, bwaj talinja nanə.
S.FORM: kinadro ka 'iyakay tacekecekelane balate saoda'ane lwapii ko alraninabee, bway talinya nane.
S.PHON: kinaɖo ka ʔijakaj taʦəkəʦəkəlanə balatə saodaʔanə lwapii ko aɭaninabəə, bwaj talinja nanə.
S.TRANSL: 他們會在村口將獵物分好，分享給族人。
They would divide the prey at the entrance of the village and share it with the tribe.

S.FORM: 'ataleese kake makavalay tatavane, ko 'angeese tatavane mwa walopomya.
S.PHON: ʔataləəsə kakə makavalaj tatavanə, ko ʔaŋəəsə tatavanə mwa walopomja.
S.FORM: 'ataleese kake makavalay tatavane, ko 'angeese tatavane mwa walopomya.
S.PHON: ʔataləəsə kakə makavalaj tatavanə, ko ʔaŋəəsə tatavanə mwa walopomja.
S.TRANSL: 我每次都會吵著爸爸，要跟著爸爸去打獵。
Every time I quarreled with my father, I wanted to go hunting with my father.

S.FORM: tatava la'apobaka ano patekedrisonga ka koameamece mosoa mya.
S.PHON: tatava laʔapobaka ano patəkəɖisoŋa ka koaməaməʦə mosoa mja.
S.FORM: tatava la'apobaka ano patekedrisonga ka koameamece mosoa mya.
S.PHON: tatava laʔapobaka ano patəkəɖisoŋa ka koaməaməʦə mosoa mja.
S.TRANSL: 爸爸說等我長大了他再帶我一起去。
Dad said that he would take me with me when I grew up.

S.FORM: tacekecekelane na sa'oalalay ka waigo'o wathii malra na bee, waigo'o paytha na sathe'ethe'ee si sata'eta'ene.
S.PHON: taʦəkəʦəkəlanə na saʔoalalaj ka waigoʔo waθii maɭa na bəə, waigoʔo pajθa na saθəʔəθəʔəə si sataʔətaʔənə.
S.FORM: tacekecekelane na sa'oalalay ka waigo'o wathii malra na bee, waigo'o paytha na sathe'ethe'ee si sata'eta'ene.
S.PHON: taʦəkəʦəkəlanə na saʔoalalaj ka waigoʔo waθii maɭa na bəə, waigoʔo pajθa na saθəʔəθəʔəə si sataʔətaʔənə.
S.TRANSL: 部落的男人也會做陷阱抓獵物，他們會製作套索陷阱和石壓式陷阱。
The men of the tribe also trap their prey, making lasso traps and rock traps.

S.FORM: tatavi nakoa ka waigo'o paytha na sathe'ethe'e, tatava ka 'apobaka, "kikay ka saakai malra na tiko'ase, paingcege kimade cengele o'ongini na bee athii made ka pa'ikay kimade ko dadaanini."
S.PHON: tatavi nakoa ka waigoʔo pajθa na saθəʔəθəʔə, tatava ka ʔapobaka, "kikaj ka saakai maɭa na tikoʔasə, paiŋʦəgə kimadə ʦəŋələ oʔoŋini na bəə aθii madə ka paʔikaj kimadə ko dadaanini."
S.FORM: tatavi nakoa ka waigo'o paytha na sathe'ethe'e, tatava ka 'apobaka, "kikay ka saakai malra na tiko'ase, paingcege kimade cengele o'ongini na bee athii made ka pa'ikay kimade ko dadaanini."
S.PHON: tatavi nakoa ka waigoʔo pajθa na saθəʔəθəʔə, tatava ka ʔapobaka, "kikaj ka saakai maɭa na tikoʔasə, paiŋʦəgə kimadə ʦəŋələ oʔoŋini na bəə aθii madə ka paʔikaj kimadə ko dadaanini."
S.TRANSL: 我的爸爸很會製作套索陷阱，爸爸說，這是用來捕抓雉雞的，我們要仔細觀察獵物的足跡，陷阱要放在獵物走的路上。

My dad is very good at making lasso traps. Dad said that this is used to catch pheasants. We have to carefully observe the footprints of the prey, and the traps should be placed on the path of the prey.

S.FORM: pwaikay koibongo 'angese na tamatama mwa walopo mya, pava'iyane kake kica'oane paytha na dreese, isipyale ko tidresili ka ladoonga malra na bee.
S.PHON: pwaikaj koiboŋo ʔaŋəsə na tamatama mwa walopo mja, pavaʔijanə kakə kiʦaʔoanə pajθa na ɖəəsə, isipjalə ko tiɖəsili ka ladooŋa maɭa na bəə.
S.FORM: pwaikay koibongo 'angese na tamatama mwa walopo mya, pava'iyane kake kica'oane paytha na dreese, isipyale ko tidresili ka ladoonga malra na bee.
S.PHON: pwaikaj koiboŋo ʔaŋəsə na tamatama mwa walopo mja, pavaʔijanə kakə kiʦaʔoanə pajθa na ɖəəsə, isipjalə ko tiɖəsili ka ladooŋa maɭa na bəə.
S.TRANSL: 為了可以趕快和大人一起去打獵，我每天練習製作陷阱，很快地我的陷阱就真的可以抓到獵物了。
In order to go hunting with adults as soon as possible, I practiced making traps every day, and soon my traps can really catch prey.

S.FORM: cengelini ki tatava ka la 'apobaka, no biilanenga ka 'isadraya koso ikamani ko wadodoonga walwalopo.
S.PHON: ʦəŋəlini ki tatava ka la ʔapobaka, no biilanəŋa ka ʔisaɖaja koso ikamani ko wadodooŋa walwalopo.

S.TRANSL: 爸爸看到後對我說我以後一定會是最會打獵的人。
Dad saw it and told me that I will be a very good hunter in the future.

S.FORM: nakaynga ka la patekedrenga kakae, laaba kake ko panaili, kisa'apaa tatavane walopo.
S.PHON: nakajŋa ka la patəkəɖəŋa kakaə, laaba kakə ko panaili, kisaʔapaa tatavanə walopo.

S.PHON: nakajŋa ka la patəkəɖəŋa kakaə, laaba kakə ko panaili, kisaʔapaa tatavanə walopo.
S.TRANSL: 現在我長大了，我背起我的弓和箭矢，跟著爸爸一起去打獵。
Now that I have grown up, I carry my bow and arrows and go hunting with my father.

S.FORM: kelayname baivi ka la alra kiname ko alraname bebee lwapii talinamiyanane.
S.PHON: kəlajnamə baivi ka la aɭa kinamə ko aɭanamə bəbəə lwapii talinamijananə.

S.TRANSL: 回村時將獵到的獵物和族人們分享。
When returning to the village, share the hunted prey with the tribe.

S.FORM: tatava ka 'apobaka, "takabeabee na sobalibalini mitya ki lamama doo kita malra 'angiyadraw na bee."
S.PHON: tatava ka ʔapobaka, "takabəabəə na sobalibalini mitja ki lamama doo kita maɭa ʔaŋijaɖaw na bəə."

S.TRANSL: 爸爸說，我們要感謝祖靈賜福，讓我們有很多獵物收穫。
Dad said, we want to thank the ancestors for their blessings, so that we have a lot of prey to harvest.

S.FORM: maeae ko mwali gako ka 'iyasolodrane kake.

S.FORM: ko mwali gako ka, maymo kake wamece na kabange.

S.FORM: ko mwali gako ka, maymo kake wamece na kabange.

S.TRANSL: 去學校的時候，我忘了帶書包。
When I was going to school, I forgot to bring my backpack.

I didn't bring anything with me.

S.TRANSL: 上課的時候，我和同學一起看課本。
During class, I and my classmates read the textbook together.

My good friend's name is tangoco, and he is also an thakongadavane child.

S.FORM: 'iyakay kiname kongadavane ta'aese salate.

S.TRANSL: 我們一起在kongadavane落讀書。
We study together in the kongadavane tribe.

S.FORM: 'atalese kiname ta'aese biciingi na maodrange timalolwai, sawkwa kiname igo'o thwakongadavadavane.

S.TRANSL: 我們常常一起聽耆老說故事，我們都很會說多納語。
We often listen to the elders telling stories together, and we both can speak the thakongadavane proficiently.

S.FORM: ma'agae kake si lakamite kake, ki tangoco ka la'aticane si lakaylai.
S.PHON: maʔagaə kakə si lakamitə kakə, ki taŋoʦo ka laʔatiʦanə si lakajlai.

S.TRANSL: 我很高也很胖，tangoco很矮也很瘦。
I am tall and overweight, while tangoco is short and thin.

tangoco is very energetic, and everyone likes him a lot.

Today was the school's sports day, and the school organized many competitions.

S.FORM: 'iyakay na mabilibilibili makakadang na malri tyaidai wayogi si wapana.

S.TRANSL: 有拔河、足球、跑步、跳舞和射箭。
There were tug of war, soccer, running, dancing, and archery.

S.FORM: kisa'apa mikake na tyaidai.
S.PHON: kisaʔapa mikakə na tjaidai.
S.FORM: kisa'apa mikake na tyaidai.
S.PHON: kisaʔapa mikakə na tjaidai.
S.TRANSL: 我比賽跑步。
I participated in the running competition.

S.FORM: ko tidayli ka, 'iyaevese kake, pakoili ka lapasawkowa 'apo'aydri.

S.TRANSL: 當我跑步的時候，我跌倒了，膝蓋流了很多血。
When I was running, I fell down, and my knees were bleeding.

S.TRANSL: 但我站起來，繼續向前跑。
But I stood up and continued running forward.

S.FORM: ko kelayli ta'iegegane ka ko lawcococao ka lakabeabee.

Everyone was happy when I reached the finish line.

S.TRANSL: 昨天我跟奶奶去市集擺攤，奶奶帶了許多自己種的蔬菜去，也製作了原住民美食給大家。
Yesterday, I went to the market with my grandmother to set up a stall. My grandmother brought a lot of vegetables that she grew herself and also prepared indigenous cuisine for everyone.

S.FORM: madalame kita takawngane ko talangalanganidra, na tavangolo si abai madrao na walangai, madrao na lawcocao na 'awkay lwavalava ko tidamadamaidra.

S.PHON: madalamə kita takawŋanə ko talaŋalaŋaniɖa, na tavaŋolo si abai maɖao na walaŋai, maɖao na lawʦoʦao na ʔawkaj lwavalava ko tidamadamaiɖa.
S.TRANSL: 大家很喜歡奶奶的攤位，辣椒和年糕很多人買，許多人來品嘗她煮的菜餚。
Everyone loved my grandmother's stall. Many people bought the chilis and rice cake, and many people came to taste the dishes she cooked.

S.TRANSL: 她煮的山蘇和糯米飯非常好吃，還有用山豬肉煮的馬告滷肉，味道吸引了很多客人，大家都來我們攤位排隊。
The ferns and sticky rice she cooked were delicious, as well as the braised pork with maqaw pepper and wild boar meat. The flavors attracted many customers, and everyone lined up at our stall.

Our goods sold out quickly, and my grandmother said, \It's all thanks to your help.\""

S.TRANSL: 這個活動，我學到了有關原住民植物和食品的知識，並且體驗到了擺攤的辛苦。
Through this event, I learned about indigenous plants and food and experienced the hard work of running a stall.

S.TRANSL: 看到了奶奶對傳統食物的熱愛，我感到非常驕傲和榮幸。
Seeing my grandmother's passion for traditional food, I feel very proud and honored.
